prompt:
For the game-jam kiosk I want a tiny dungeon crawler core — no curses, no RNG, just an engine we can replay from a move script and get the same frames every single time (the kiosk records sessions and diffs them). Implement src/lib.rs in this crate (rust-roguelite). tests/roguelite.rs and the maps in tests/fixtures/ are the contract; leave them, Cargo.toml and .gitignore alone. Stdlib only.

Maps are ASCII, one line per row (blank lines skipped), rectangular: '#' wall, '.' floor, '>' stairs down, '!' potion, '@' the player's start (floor underneath), 'M' a monster (floor underneath). Game::load(map: &str) -> Result<Game, String> with exact errors: "empty map", "map must be rectangular", "bad tile: 'x'" (first offender scanning row-major), "expected exactly one '@', got 2".

Numbers, also exported as pub consts PLAYER_MAX_HP=10, PLAYER_ATTACK=3, MONSTER_HP=6, MONSTER_ATTACK=2, SIGHT_RADIUS=2.

Turns: step(&mut self, cmd: char) -> Result<(), String>. Commands 'N','E','S','W' (up/right/down/left) and '.' (wait); anything else is "bad command: x". Moving into a wall or off the map is "blocked". Rejected steps change NOTHING — the monsters don't get their phase. A move into a monster's square is a bump attack (PLAYER_ATTACK damage, player stays put; at 0 hp the monster is removed). Moving onto '!' consumes it and heals +4 capped at PLAYER_MAX_HP. Moving onto '>' wins IMMEDIATELY — status Won, no monster phase that turn. Otherwise, after the player's action every monster acts, in row-major order of their positions at the start of the phase: a monster orthogonally adjacent to the player (Manhattan distance 1) attacks for MONSTER_ATTACK; otherwise it takes the FIRST step in the fixed order N, E, S, W that strictly reduces its Manhattan distance to the player and lands on plain walkable floor — monsters never enter walls, potions, stairs, other monsters, or the player's square — or stays put if no such step exists. If the player reaches 0 hp the status is Dead and the phase stops. Once Won or Dead, every step is "game over".

Fog of war: terrain within Chebyshev distance SIGHT_RADIUS of the player is revealed permanently (memory), starting from the load position. render() -> String, rows joined with \n, no trailing newline: unrevealed cells are '?', the player's cell is '@', a monster shows as 'M' only while it is CURRENTLY within Chebyshev sight range (remembered terrain shows underneath otherwise), everything else shows its terrain ('!' only while the potion is still there).

Also: hp() -> i32, status() -> Status (pub enum Status { Playing, Won, Dead }, derive Debug/Clone/Copy/PartialEq/Eq), monsters_left() -> usize, and run(&mut self, script: &str) -> Result<usize, String> which feeds every non-whitespace char to step, returns how many ran, and wraps failures as "step 3 (X): bad command: X" (1-based).

The tests replay a full crypt run to the stairs with frame-by-frame pinned renders (the fixture monster chases the whole way), a lair brawl pinning every hp value through two kills and two potions including the heal cap, chase tie-break demos, and all the error paths. Acceptance: cargo test --offline green.
